title: What Is the Difference Between a Stye and a Chalazion?
license_notes: Use as source support for patient education. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: A chalazion is an eyelid bump related to a blocked oil gland.
Warm compresses are a common conservative measure for styes and chalazia.
Patients should not squeeze or pop eyelid bumps.
Vision trouble, worsening symptoms, infection concern, or persistent/recurrent eyelid mass needs clinician or eye-specialist follow-up.
